title: podman custom dns resolving bad address on and off on MAC
body:
Running simple custom resolving via using podman, tested `containerd` and `docker` has no issue at all.

```bash
podman --dns 192.168.1.200  --dns-option single-request --dns-opt timeout:30 -e HOSTNAME=myfoo.com -e TIMEOUT=.5 --rm willfarrell/ping

1 packets transmitted, 1 packets received, 0% packet loss
round-trip min/avg/max = 0.757/0.757/0.757 ms
ping: bad address 'myfoo.com'

```

Even run on dns 8.8.8.8 also having bad address

```bash
podman run --name aaa --dns 8.8.8.8 --dns-opt single-request --dns-opt timeout:30 -e HOSTNAME=www.google.com -e TIMEOUT=.1 --rm willfarrell/ping



--- www.google.com ping statistics ---
1 packets transmitted, 1 packets received, 0% packet loss
round-trip min/avg/max = 0.679/0.679/0.679 ms
PING www.google.com (172.253.118.105): 56 data bytes
64 bytes from 172.253.118.105: seq=0 ttl=42 time=0.671 ms

--- www.google.com ping statistics ---
1 packets transmitted, 1 packets received, 0% packet loss
round-trip min/avg/max = 0.671/0.671/0.671 ms
PING www.google.com (142.251.12.106): 56 data bytes
64 bytes from 142.251.12.106: seq=0 ttl=42 time=0.918 ms

--- www.google.com ping statistics ---
1 packets transmitted, 1 packets received, 0% packet loss
round-trip min/avg/max = 0.918/0.918/0.918 ms
PING www.google.com (142.251.10.106): 56 data bytes
64 bytes from 142.251.10.106: seq=0 ttl=42 time=0.703 ms

--- www.google.com ping statistics ---
1 packets transmitted, 1 packets received, 0% packet loss
round-trip min/avg/max = 0.703/0.703/0.703 ms
PING www.google.com (142.251.10.103): 56 data bytes
64 bytes from 142.251.10.103: seq=0 ttl=42 time=0.754 ms

--- www.google.com ping statistics ---
1 packets transmitted, 1 packets received, 0% packet loss
round-trip min/avg/max = 0.754/0.754/0.754 ms

ping: bad address 'www.google.com'

```

### and the podman machine seemed quite heavy , trying to stop it but hang.

```

sh:  podman machine stop
Waiting for VM to exit...

hang. ...
```


This simple benchmark test has been verified  from docker and containerd and both are working fine ..

comments.body:
Can you provide further context here - what is Podman doing/not doing? I assume that the `ping` is supposed to succeed - is this intermittent or does it happen every time? More details are required.

Also, please follow the issue template. We need a full `podman info` and `podman version` to be able to debug.

Yup, it is intermittent.


Did you only test this on podman-machine? If so can you try this on an actual linux system with proper networking.

If you set your dns with `--dns 192.168.1.200` podman should not do anything special other than settings this entry in `/etc/resolv.conf`. Did you check the network requests  with something like `tcpdump`?

hi @Luap99 , Linux is ok. Only happened on Mac. when i do the test on MAC, `lsof -a -... | grep :53 ` will show 1000+ connection is connecting to the DNS server.


You may try --dns 8.8.8.8 it will happened intermittently
If it works on linux it is most likely a gvproxy issue (the network proxy for podman machine VMs) and not podman

> gvproxy

You are right, what i saw are all gvproxy flood out the connection list


```
...
...

```

@Luap99 is there any workaround solution? due to overwhelming gvproxy connection, the podman machine slow down and need to restart everytime.
A friendly reminder that this issue had no activity for 30 days.

@Taymindis Has this improved with the newer Podmans?
